patient.query: Yeah, it's like one moment, I’m okay, and then the next, I’m drowning in all the memories. I keep thinking about what went wrong, why it had to end. It’s just so frustrating because I want to move on, but it feels like I keep getting pulled back.

triggers: thinking about why it had to end
automatic_thoughts: I want to move on
I keep getting pulled back

patient.query: It’s like this aching nostalgia, you know? I start remembering the good times, and it makes me feel so lonely. Then I spiral, feeling like maybe I should have done something different to make it work. It’s exhausting to keep wrestling with those thoughts and emotions.

patient.query: Yeah, it really is tough. When the memories come up, I feel this mix of sadness and regret. Like, I can’t help but wish things had turned out differently. Sometimes, I even feel this anger, not just at the situation but at myself for not seeing things clearly before. It’s like I’m stuck in this loop of what-ifs.
